element.name: Air pressure characteristic (3 h)
element.description: Characteristic of the pressure tendency over the last three hours given by code 0-8, indicating whether pressure is rising, falling or steady.

element.name: Indicator for type of station and type of weather information (SYNOP)
element.description: Indicator for type of station operation (manned or automatic) for the station generating the SYNOP message. Also includes information about whether present and past weather data is included in the message and which codetables have been used for past and present weather. Uses the WMO code table 1860

element.name: Cloud cover, lowest clouds
element.description: Amount of all low clouds (CL), or -amount of middle clouds (CM) if there are no low clouds. It is registered using a code 0 - 8 describing how many eights of the sky are covered by low clouds (0 = no clouds, 8 = completely overcast. Code -3 or 9 = cloud cover can not be estimated because the sky is obstructed from view because of fog, drifting snow etc. Code -3 is presented as .).

element.name: Cloud cover
element.description: Total cloud cover is registered using a code 0 - 8 describing how many eights of the sky are covered by clouds (0 = no clouds, 8 = completely overcast. Code -3 or 9 = cloud cover can not be estimated because the sky is obstructed from view because of fog, drifting snow etc. Code -3 is presented as .).

element.name: Type of low clouds
element.description: Cloud type, low clouds (base 0 - approx 2500 meter), by code 0 - 9 (Cu, Cb, St, Sc, ref cloud atlas).

element.name: Type of medium clouds
element.description: Cloud type, medium clouds (base approx 2500-6000 meter), by code 0 - 9 (As, Ac, Ns, ref cloud atlas).

element.name: Present weather
element.description: Weather at observation time, given as synoptic code 00-99

element.name: Prominent weather
element.description: Highest code for weather (code 0 - 9). Timespan of this observation depends on time of day: At reference time 00, 06, 12, 18 UTC: over last 6 hours. At reference time 03, 09, 15, 21 UTC: over last 3 hours. At reference time 01,02,04,05,07 … 22,23 UTC: over last hour.
calculationmethods: A method to describe the observation of what has happened since last observation, often used for type of weather.

element.name: Second prominent weather
element.description: Second highest code for weather (code 0 - 9). Timespan of this observation depends on time of day: At reference time 00, 06, 12, 18 UTC: over last 6 hours. At reference time 03, 09, 15, 21 UTC: over last 3 hours. At reference time 01,02,04,05,07 … 22,23 UTC: over last hour.
calculationmethods: A method to describe the observation of what has happened since last observation, often used for type of weather.

element.name: Cloud base height
element.description: Height of lowest cloud base, reported with code, converted to meters. Code -3 = cloud cover can not be estimated due to fog, drifting snow etc. Code -3 is presented as '.'  (in synop code=9))

element.name: Wind direction
element.description: Mean wind direction over the last ten minutes before the observation time. Wind direction is defined as the direction from which the wind blows and is registered in degrees, where 360 degrees is north and 90 degrees is east.

element.name: Wind direction of maximum mean wind speed (1 h)
element.description: Wind direction at maximum mean wind speed over the last hour
calculationmethods: Frå-retninga som tilhøyrer maksimal vindhastigheit i den gitte perioden

element.name: Mean wind speed
element.description: Mean wind speed is registered as a mean value of the wind speed over the last ten minutes before the observation time. (default: 10 meters above ground, some stations have measurements at 2 meters)

element.name: Maximum wind gust (10 min)
element.description: Maximum wind gust for the last ten minutes
calculationmethods: Maximum value observed or calculated in the given period

element.name: Maximum mean wind speed (1 h)
element.description: Hourly maximum mean wind speed
calculationmethods: Maximum value observed or calculated in the given period

element.name: Maximum wind gust (1 h)
element.description: Hourly maximum wind gust
calculationmethods: Maximum value observed or calculated in the given period

element.name: Precipitation in gauge
element.description: Total precipitation amount in gauge (accumulated since last emptying). Timing for emptying and algoritm for calculating the precipitation amount depends on sensortype
calculationmethods: Accumulated from 0, which means the sum over an unknown period.

element.name: Precipitation (1 h)
element.description: Amount of precipitation per hour
calculationmethods: The arithmetic sum of data values or accumulated value in a given period.

element.name: Sunshine duration (1 h)
element.description: Number of minutes of sunshine over the last hour.
calculationmethods: The arithmetic sum of data values or accumulated value in a given period.

element.name: Number of minutes with precipitation during the last hour
element.description: Number of minutes where precipitation has been detected during the last 10 minutes.
calculationmethods: The arithmetic sum of data values or accumulated value in a given period.

element.name: Air pressure at station level
element.description: Air pressure at the station. The parameter is usually called QFE in aviation and shows the measured air pressure reduced to the reference height of the station.

element.name: Baromeric tendency (3 h)
element.description: Barometer tendency, i.e. change over the last three hours
calculationmethods: A method to describe the observation of what has happened since last observation, often used for type of weather.

element.name: Air pressure at sea level
element.description: Air pressure reduced to mean sea level. The parameter is usually called QFF in aviation and shows the measured air pressure reduced to mean sea level by applying actual atmospheric conditions.

element.name: Mean reflected longwave radiation (1 h)
element.description: Mean reflected longwave radiation per hour. The part of the longwave radiation emitted by the Earth that is reflected back again by the atmosphere. The instrument measures the downwelling radiation flux through a horizontal surface (W/m2).
calculationmethods: Standard arithmetic mean value for the given period

element.name: Air temperature
element.description: Air temperature (default 2 m above ground), present value

element.name: Minimum air temperature (1 h)
element.description: Lowest recorded air temperature per hour
calculationmethods: Minimum value observed or calculated in the given period

element.name: Maximum air temperature (1 h)
element.description: Highest recorded air temperature per hour
calculationmethods: Maximum value observed or calculated in the given period

element.name: Relative air humidity
element.description: Relative humidity

element.name: Visibility
element.description: Visibility. Converted from synop code (max 75 km) or metar code (max 10 km).

element.name: Battery voltage
element.description: Voltage on station battery

element.name: Time of maximum wind gust (1 h)
element.description: Time of maximum wind gust occurrence (time format: hhmm)
calculationmethods: A method to describe the observation of what has happened since last observation, often used for type of weather.

element.name: Time of maximum mean wind speed (1 h)
element.description: Time of maximum wind speed occurrence (time format: hhmm)
calculationmethods: A method to describe the observation of what has happened since last observation, often used for type of weather.

element.name: Mean global radiation (1 t)
element.description: Hourly mean global radiation. Global radiation is the total downwelling shortwave radiation from the sun. Shortwave radiation have wavelengths in the area 295-2800 nm and therefore includes ultraviolet, visible and infrared light. The instrument measures the radiation flux through a horizontal surface (W/m2).
calculationmethods: Standard arithmetic mean value for the given period

element.name: Mean global radiation (1 min)
element.description: Mean global radiation over the last minute. Global radiation is the total downwelling shortwave radiation from the sun. Shortwave radiation have wavelengths in the area 295-2800 nm and therefore includes ultraviolet, visible and infrared light. The instrument measures the radiation flux through a horizontal surface (W/m2).
calculationmethods: Standard arithmetic mean value for the given period

element.name: Mean reflected longwave radiation (1 min)
element.description: Mean reflected longwave radiation per minute. The part of the longwave radiation emitted by the Earth that is reflected back again by the atmosphere. The instrument measures the downwelling radiation flux through a horizontal surface (W/m2).
calculationmethods: Standard arithmetic mean value for the given period

element.name: Mean difference between downwelling longwave flux and instrument longwave radiation  (1 min)
element.description: 1 minute mean of the difference between the downwelling longwave radiation detected and the longwave radiation emitted by the instrument. Not corrected for instrument temperature
calculationmethods: Standard arithmetic mean value for the given period

element.name: Sunshine duration (1 min)
element.description: Sunshine duration over the last minute.
calculationmethods: The arithmetic sum of data values or accumulated value in a given period.

element.name: Instrument temperature longwave radiation
element.description: Instrument temperature longwave radiation

element.name: Instrument temperature shortwave radiation
element.description: Instrument temperature shortwave radiation
